documentation.value: Prints a string or a list of strings to the selected output handle (default STDOUT). Returns true on success.
A list of strings to print. Without arguments, prints $_.
Removes the trailing newline from a string (if present). Returns the number of characters removed.
The string or variable to remove the trailing newline from. Modifies the variable in-place.
Splits a string into a list of substrings based on a delimiter pattern.
The regex pattern or string delimiter to split on (e.g., /,/, /\s+/).
The string to split. Defaults to $_ if omitted.
Optional maximum number of fields. If negative, treated as arbitrarily large.
Joins the elements of a list into a single string with the specified separator.
The string to place between each element.
The list of values to join.
Appends one or more values to the end of an array. Returns the new number of elements.
The array to append to.
The values to push onto the array.
Removes and returns the last element of an array, shortening the array by one.
The array to pop from. Defaults to @_ inside subroutines.
Removes and returns the first element of an array, shifting all other elements down by one.
The array to shift from. Defaults to @ARGV outside subroutines, @_ inside.
Prepends one or more values to the beginning of an array. Returns the new number of elements.
The array to prepend to.
The values to add to the beginning of the array.
Returns the length in characters of the value of EXPR.
The string expression. Defaults to $_ if omitted.
Extracts a substring from a string. Can optionally replace the extracted portion.
The source string.
The 0-based starting position. Negative values count from the end.
Optional. The number of characters. Negative values leave that many characters at the end.
Optional. A string to replace the extracted portion with.
Returns the position of the first occurrence of SUBSTR in STRING. Returns -1 if not found.
The string to search in.
The substring to search for.
Optional. The position to start searching from (0-based).
Opens a file and associates it with a filehandle. Returns true on success.
The filehandle to associate (e.g., my $fh or a bareword).
The open mode: '<' (read), '>' (write), '>>' (append), '+<' (read/write).
The path to the file to open.
Closes an opened filehandle. Returns true on success.
The filehandle to close.
Raises an exception. Outside an eval, prints the message to STDERR and exits with the current $! value.
The error message or object to die with. If it doesn't end in a newline, file and line are appended.
Prints a warning message to STDERR but does not exit the program.
The warning message. If it doesn't end in a newline, file and line number are appended.
Returns a formatted string (like printf but returns the string instead of printing it).
The format string with printf-style specifiers (%s, %d, %f, %x, etc.).
The values to substitute into the format string.
